tools_used: direct fetch of a supremecourt.gov docket PDF + local pypdf text extraction
helpful: Provisioned document texts (QP, petition, BIO) — carried most of the analysis
Statpack relist/CVSG/per-Term cuts as probability anchors
Snapshot's docket-entry PDF URLs, which let me fetch the June 2026 supplemental brief that explained the hold
gaps: Corpus query sidecar at 127.0.0.1:8377 was unreachable, so `fedcourts query` priors were unavailable this run
Statpack lacks a CFR (call-for-response) cut — the single strongest docket signal here has no in-repo base rate
The prompt's 'Segment base rate by salience band' table is absent from the committed statpack

Provisioned text for supplemental briefs (the pipeline fetched petition/BIO but not the later filing that turned out to be pivotal); WebFetch is 403-blocked on supremecourt.gov, so this needed a manual curl + pypdf
notes: Forward cell; hold-for-Pung dynamics made docket-timing signals (CFR, hold, surviving the post-Pung cleanup) more informative than corpus priors, so the sidecar outage cost little this run.
